Prompt
U
Oracle Events
Search emails for NorthWind and Pam Kowalski using search_emails (query: "Pam Kowalski" or "NorthWind" or similar). Discover Pam Kowalski's April 24 escalation email sent directly to David Chen, CC Victor Huang, stating that NorthWind is evaluating whether MoveOps can continue to support the account. She cites two incidents: Raj Patel's server lab was loaded before the required IT escort arrived and was then placed in overnight warehouse storage in breach of explicit no-storage instructions, and Emilia Cruz's Steinway piano was damaged during extraction with inadequate follow-up. She also states that Catalina committed to a recovery plan on April 14 that was never delivered. Pam requests a retention proposal by end of day Friday. Also discover David's April 24 internal team email requesting specific input from Chloe, Julian, Marcus, Catalina, and Alejandro for the retention response.
Search emails for Victor Huang and the chain-of-custody breach using search_emails (query: "Victor Huang" or "chain of custody" or "server lab" or similar). Discover Victor Huang's original email to Catalina laying out 5 non-negotiable security requirements for Raj Patel's server transport, including data preparation and encryption verification, IT security escort, no warehouse stops, GPS tracking, and chain-of-custody log. Also discover the breach report documenting that the equipment was loaded before the escort arrived and placed in overnight warehouse storage, violating at least two of those requirements.
Search emails for Emilia Cruz piano damage using search_emails (query: "Emilia" or "piano" or "Steinway" or "KeyMove" or similar). Discover correspondence about the Steinway Model B grand piano being damaged during a stairwell extraction. Find Marcus Thorne's email flagging a $1,200 KeyMove insurance claim rider tied to the damage, and his note that this creates both a margin problem and a retention problem on NorthWind alongside the Raj Patel chain-of-custody issue.
Search emails for Catalina's communications with Pam about the NorthWind Denver expansion using search_emails or get_thread (query: "Catalina" and "NorthWind" or "Denver" or "recovery" or similar). Discover Catalina's April 14 email to Pam promising a comprehensive service recovery plan within the week covering the Raj Patel chain-of-custody breakdown, the Emilia Cruz damage claim, and concrete service changes. This is the unfulfilled commitment Pam referenced in her final warning. Also discover Catalina's April 1 reply to Pam's Denver expansion request, in which she acknowledges Q1 apartment quality issues and her slow response times while promising personal coordination of both Denver moves. Also discover the prior escalation where Pam went directly to David.
Search emails for NorthWind financial and retention analysis using search_emails (query: "retention model" or "NorthWind" or "discount scenario" or similar). Discover Alejandro Fuentes' draft retention model email to Marcus with scenario analysis: Scenario A holds current pricing with low credibility, and Scenario B offers a 12% Q3 discount on relocation services tied to full Denver expansion commitment (minimum 18 employee relocations in Q3). Also find Marcus's analysis of the combined margin and retention risk from the piano claim and chain-of-custody breach.
Search Slack for NorthWind discussions using conversations_search_messages (query: "NorthWind" or "Pam" or "retention" or "chain of custody" or similar). Find internal discussions in #customer-engagement (C002) and #operations (C006) about the chain-of-custody breach timeline, the piano damage incident, Catalina's handling of the account, and David's posts coordinating the retention response.
Search CRM for the NorthWind company record and deals using crm_search_companies (name: "NorthWind" or similar) and crm_search_deals. Find the NorthWind company record (company_northwind), the original $60,000 enterprise relocation deal (closedWon), and the Denver satellite office expansion deal at $14,700 with stage contractSent. The CRM deal stages do not yet reflect the retention crisis.
Search CRM for NorthWind engagement history using crm_list_engagements with company_ids including the NorthWind company_id. Find any existing engagement notes documenting prior client interactions and the current account trail.
Search Linear for NorthWind-related tickets using linear_list_issues (query: "NorthWind" or "Raj Patel" or "Emilia Cruz" or similar). Find relocation tracking tickets for the Denver expansion moves and any open issues related to the chain-of-custody breach, piano damage, or remediation work.
Search Airtable relocation records for NorthWind using airtable_search_records (base_id, table_name: "Relocations" or similar, field_name: "Company" or similar, value: "NorthWind" or similar). Find relocation records for both Denver moves — Raj Patel (recRajPatelChicagoDenver, In Progress) and Emilia Cruz (recEmiliaCruzChicagoDenver, In Progress) — with current statuses, coordinator assignments (Blessing Okafor), and special requirements.
Search QuickBooks for NorthWind invoices and bills using quickbooks_search_invoices (criteria referencing NorthWind or similar) and quickbooks_search_bills. Find NorthWind-related financial records including the original $60,000 invoice (INV-2026-003), the Denver expansion invoice at $14,700 (INV-2026-0412), and the KeyMove insurance claim bill at $1,200 (BILL-KEYMOVE-2026-0417) to assess total financial exposure and outstanding balances.
Search emails for StormCloud and Liam Park using search_emails (query: "StormCloud" or "Liam Park" or "Liam" or similar). Discover Liam Park's April 24 escalation email to Mina listing four complaints: Jae-won Kim's Austin apartment was never finalized with UrbanNest despite being told it was his permanent placement, the promised goodwill credit never appeared on their invoice, Simone Richter's desk reimbursement is stuck with no timeline, and Anya Petrova's warm introduction to Ridgeline Robotics went unanswered. Also discover Mina's internal reconstruction email to the team and her formal response to Liam with specific commitments on each item.
Search Slack for StormCloud discussions using conversations_search_messages (query: "StormCloud" or "Liam" or "Jae-won" or "Ridgeline" or similar). Find Mina's detailed reconstruction post in #executive (C004, April 25) tracing each failure chain. Find David Chen's April 25 message in #customer-engagement (C002) admitting that he celebrated Anya's Ridgeline referral in Slack but never turned it into a live deal, and that Kenji Watanabe's follow-up email was still sitting unanswered. Find Mina's recovery plan post in #executive (C004) with six commitments: reopen Jae-won housing, extend temp housing if lease cannot execute, correct Airtable and Linear records, process the $500 goodwill credit, document Simone's stipend as valid-but-held, and reply to Kenji to recover the Ridgeline referral.
Search CRM for StormCloud using crm_search_companies (name: "StormCloud" or similar) and crm_search_deals. Find the StormCloud company record (company_stormcloud) and the closedWon deal at $26,400 to assess the account value and current deal stage.
Search Linear for StormCloud tickets using linear_list_issues (query: "StormCloud" or "Jae-won" or similar). Find the StormCloud account incident reconstruction ticket (issue number 284, assigned to Mina Hashimoto, priority 1, due April 24) with a detailed description tracing the confirmed failure chain across Jae-won's housing, the support knock-on effect, the stipend hold impact, and the referral drop.
Read Linear comments on the StormCloud reconstruction ticket using linear_list_comments. Find the client-facing recovery plan comment documenting specific commitments Mina sent to Liam, including housing reopening, temp housing extension, record corrections, goodwill credit applied to INV-2026-0319, and Ridgeline follow-up.
Search Airtable for StormCloud relocation records using airtable_search_records (base_id, table_name: "Relocations" or similar, field_name: "Company" or similar, value: "StormCloud" or similar). Find Jae-won Kim's relocation record (recJaeWonKimStormCloudAustin), which has been corrected to "In Progress" status as of April 24 after previously being incorrectly marked as Completed/Confirmed. Check other StormCloud relocation records for current statuses.
Search QuickBooks for StormCloud invoices using quickbooks_search_invoices (criteria referencing StormCloud or similar). Find the StormCloud invoice at $26,400 (INV-2026-002) and confirm that no $500 goodwill credit appears on the invoice. Cross-referencing with the Linear reconstruction comments and Marcus Thorne's financial review reveals the credit was misapplied to cancelled estimate EST-2026-0287 instead of active invoice INV-2026-0319.
Look up Pam Kowalski's contact information using crm_search_contacts (full_name: "Pam Kowalski" or similar). Find her CRM contact record (contact_pam_kowalski) with email pam.kowalski@northwindtech.com.
Check David Chen's calendar for the current and coming week using calendar_get_calendar_events_from_to to identify an available time slot for the retention call with Pam.
Update the NorthWind Denver expansion deal stage using crm_update_deal_stage to reflect that the account is at risk. The deal_id for the active Denver deal should be updated from its current stage (contractSent) to an at-risk or equivalent stage.
Update the StormCloud deal stage using crm_update_deal_stage to reflect that the account is at risk. The deal_id for the closedWon deal (deal_stormcloud_won, $26,400) should be updated to an at-risk or equivalent stage based on the CFO escalation and multiple unresolved service failures.
Create a CRM engagement note for NorthWind using crm_create_engagement (engagement_type: "NOTE", company_ids including the NorthWind company_id). The note should document: the chain-of-custody breach on Raj Patel's server lab, the Emilia Cruz piano damage and $1,200 insurance rider, Catalina's unfulfilled April 14 recovery commitment, Pam's April 24 final warning and retention plan deadline, financial exposure across the $14,700 Denver deal and the original $60,000 engagement, and Alejandro's retention scenario analysis.
Create a CRM engagement note for StormCloud using crm_create_engagement documenting: Liam Park's April 24 escalation with four specific complaints (Jae-won housing, goodwill credit, Simone's stipend, Ridgeline referral), Mina's recovery response and commitments, David's admission of the Ridgeline referral drop, and the current status of each open item.
Create Linear tickets for any unresolved items on either account that do not already have tracking using linear_create_issue. Candidates include items from the NorthWind retention response that lack tickets, such as the formal retention plan delivery to Pam, or any StormCloud commitments not yet captured in existing tracking.
Send an email from david.chen@moveops.com to pam.kowalski@northwindtech.com using send_email. The email should acknowledge the service gaps on the Denver expansion without blaming any specific internal team member, reference the chain-of-custody and piano damage incidents as things David takes seriously, express commitment to a concrete plan, and request a call this week to walk through the retention proposal in person.
Create a calendar event for the retention call with Pam using calendar_add_calendar_event. The event should be scheduled for an available slot during the current or coming week, with david.chen@moveops.com and pam.kowalski@northwindtech.com as attendees, and a title and description referencing the NorthWind account retention discussion.
Post a summary message in #customer-engagement (channel C002) using conversations_add_message. The message should brief Mina, Catalina, and Emeka on the current state of both at-risk accounts: what issues were found on NorthWind (chain-of-custody breach, piano damage, Catalina's unfulfilled promise, Pam's retention deadline) and StormCloud (housing failure, referral drop, stipend hold, goodwill credit), what actions have been taken (CRM updates, engagement notes, email to Pam, call scheduled, tickets created), and what each AM should be prepared for if either client reaches out directly.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.
universe_idmcp-advanced-3-20-moveops-metadata

Oracle Events
Search emails for NorthWind and Pam Kowalski using search_emails (query: "Pam Kowalski" or "NorthWind" or similar). Discover Pam Kowalski's April 24 escalation email sent directly to David Chen, CC Victor Huang, stating that NorthWind is evaluating whether MoveOps can continue to support the account. She cites two incidents: Raj Patel's server lab was loaded before the required IT escort arrived and was then placed in overnight warehouse storage in breach of explicit no-storage instructions, and Emilia Cruz's Steinway piano was damaged during extraction with inadequate follow-up. She also states that Catalina committed to a recovery plan on April 14 that was never delivered. Pam requests a retention proposal by end of day Friday. Also discover David's April 24 internal team email requesting specific input from Chloe, Julian, Marcus, Catalina, and Alejandro for the retention response.
Search emails for Victor Huang and the chain-of-custody breach using search_emails (query: "Victor Huang" or "chain of custody" or "server lab" or similar). Discover Victor Huang's original email to Catalina laying out 5 non-negotiable security requirements for Raj Patel's server transport, including data preparation and encryption verification, IT security escort, no warehouse stops, GPS tracking, and chain-of-custody log. Also discover the breach report documenting that the equipment was loaded before the escort arrived and placed in overnight warehouse storage, violating at least two of those requirements.
Search emails for Emilia Cruz piano damage using search_emails (query: "Emilia" or "piano" or "Steinway" or "KeyMove" or similar). Discover correspondence about the Steinway Model B grand piano being damaged during a stairwell extraction. Find Marcus Thorne's email flagging a $1,200 KeyMove insurance claim rider tied to the damage, and his note that this creates both a margin problem and a retention problem on NorthWind alongside the Raj Patel chain-of-custody issue.
Search emails for Catalina's communications with Pam about the NorthWind Denver expansion using search_emails or get_thread (query: "Catalina" and "NorthWind" or "Denver" or "recovery" or similar). Discover Catalina's April 14 email to Pam promising a comprehensive service recovery plan within the week covering the Raj Patel chain-of-custody breakdown, the Emilia Cruz damage claim, and concrete service changes. This is the unfulfilled commitment Pam referenced in her final warning. Also discover Catalina's April 1 reply to Pam's Denver expansion request, in which she acknowledges Q1 apartment quality issues and her slow response times while promising personal coordination of both Denver moves. Also discover the prior escalation where Pam went directly to David.
Search emails for NorthWind financial and retention analysis using search_emails (query: "retention model" or "NorthWind" or "discount scenario" or similar). The event should be scheduled for an available slot during the current or coming week, with david.chen@moveops.com and pam.kowalski@northwindtech.com as attendees, and a title and description referencing the NorthWind account retention discussion.
Post a summary message in #customer-engagement (channel C002) using conversations_add_message. The message should brief Mina, Catalina, and Emeka on the current state of both at-risk accounts: what issues were found on NorthWind (chain-of-custody breach, piano damage, Catalina's unfulfilled promise, Pam's retention deadline) and StormCloud (housing failure, referral drop, stipend hold, goodwill credit), what actions have been taken (CRM updates, engagement notes, email to Pam, call scheduled, tickets created), and what each AM should be prepared for if either client reaches out directly.

These details appear in the CRM engagement note but not in the final agent response.
